2014-02-01.title: Give Malta Your Tired and Huddled, and Rich
2014-02-01.abstract: A Mediterranean island is in search of wealthy foreigners with the means to buy citizenship and bring the government some cash.

2014-02-01.title: France and Britain Agree on Outer Space, but Find No Common Ground on Europe
2014-02-01.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain wants to revise the European Union’s rule book, but President François Hollande of France says no.

2014-02-01.title: Even Out of Office, a Wielder of Great Power in Yemen
2014-02-01.abstract: Ali Abdullah Saleh, Yemen’s former president, is a rarity in the Arab world: an autocrat overthrown by popular revolt who nonetheless remains in his country, undisturbed.

2014-02-01.title: On 35th Anniversary of Diplomatic Ties With U.S., China Tries to Soften Image
2014-02-01.abstract: China has moved to expand friendly cultural exchanges with the United States and promote a series of prominent collaborations in music, dance and education, particularly in New York.

2014-02-01.title: Obama Is Said to Plan Fence-Mending Trip to Saudi Arabia
2014-02-01.abstract: An official said President Obama would seek to reassure King Abdullah of Saudi Arabia after a period of tension over American policies toward Iran and Syria.

2014-02-02.title: Relocating Traditions in China
2014-02-02.abstract: Village-based traditions once practiced by close-living families and neighbors are disappearing in an increasingly urban China.

2014-02-02.title: In China, ‘Once the Villages Are Gone, the Culture Is Gone’
2014-02-02.abstract: Rapid urbanization in China means village life, the bedrock of Chinese culture, is rapidly vanishing, and with it, traditions and history.

2014-02-02.title: Spurred by Global Crises, Germany Weighs a More Muscular Foreign Policy
2014-02-02.abstract: Some German leaders are even suggesting that the country should no longer reflexively avoid some military deployments, as it did in Libya almost three years ago.

2014-02-02.title: His Friends Know Him as Petroswickonicovick
2014-02-02.abstract: Carrying an extraordinary name is remarkably widespread in Brazil. Just ask Wonarllevyston Garlan Marllon Branddon Bruno Paullynelly Mell Oliveira Pereira.

2014-02-02.title: Protesters Disrupt Thai Voting, Forcing Additional Elections
2014-02-02.abstract: Opposition forces vowed to contest the results of Sunday’s election, in which their efforts to block polling sites mostly affected Bangkok and areas surrounding it.

2014-02-03.title: Palestinian Leader Seeks NATO Force in Future State
2014-02-03.abstract: Palestine, President Mahmoud Abbas said, would not have its own army, only a police force, so the NATO mission would prevent the weapons smuggling and terrorism that Israel fears.

2014-02-03.title: Sudan Tells Red Cross to Halt Work
2014-02-03.abstract: The government determined that the group, which helps more than a million people in Sudan, had not fulfilled unspecified conditions for its aid efforts, the state-run news agency said.

2014-02-03.title: Kerry and Iran Minister Confer on Nuclear Issue
2014-02-03.abstract: Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif said Iran wanted to negotiate seriously, and he offered to begin a dialogue on human rights issues with the European Union.

2014-02-03.title: Syria Hits Areas Held by Rebels in Aleppo
2014-02-03.abstract: Makeshift bombs were dropped across the south and east of the country’s largest city, killing at least three dozen people, most of them women and children, antigovernment activists said.

2014-02-03.title: As Ukraine’s President Returns From Leave, His Options Seem Dismal
2014-02-03.abstract: President Viktor F. Yanukovych may need to choose between a bloody suppression of protesters or a surrender of authority that could leave him as president but empower his opponents.

2014-02-04.title: Iran Giving Food Away to Millions for Relief
2014-02-04.abstract: The authorities began the initiative, which drew long lines, both to help those with low incomes and to try to lower inflation on food prices, local news media reported.

2014-02-04.title: Tacloban Three Months Later
2014-02-04.abstract: Nearly three months after Typhoon Haiyan devastated the Philippines, many still live in tents with little electricity. Keith Bradsher reports from Tacloban.

2014-02-04.title: Russia: 3 Jailed in Anti-Gay Case
2014-02-04.abstract: A court in Russia’s Far East on Monday found three men guilty of murdering a man in an attack that prosecutors said was motivated by homophobia.

2014-02-04.title: Britain: French Comic Barred
2014-02-04.abstract: The controversial French comedian Dieudonné M’Bala M’Bala has been barred from entering Britain because of his extreme views, the Home Office said Monday.

2014-02-04.title: Born While Floods Surge
2014-02-04.abstract: Yolando was born during the worst of typhoon Haiyan. He lives with his family in a region devastated by the storm.

2014-02-05.title: Pakistani Side Fails to Show Up at Taliban Peace Talks
2014-02-05.abstract: The failure of the government representatives to appear for the talks is the latest episode in a process that has been characterized by confusion, contradiction and delays.

2014-02-05.title: German Paper Says U.S. Kept Tabs on Leader
2014-02-05.abstract: The newspaper, Süddeutsche Zeitung, reported that American intelligence services began monitoring Chancellor Gerhard Schröder after he opposed the Bush administration’s plan to go to war in Iraq.

2014-02-05.title: Where N.S.A. Kept Watch in Cold War, Artists Now Find Refuge
2014-02-05.abstract: More than two decades after the United States pulled up its final cables from Field Station Berlin, the ruins of the complex still hold a mystical attraction for history buffs, artists and tourists.

2014-02-05.title: On Top of Devil's Mountain
2014-02-05.abstract: Teufelsberg, or Devil’s Mountain, includes an abandoned Cold War N.S.A. listening post in Berlin that has evolved into a de facto art installation.

2014-02-05.title: U.S. Representative to Syrian Opposition Is Retiring
2014-02-05.abstract: As the American ambassador in Damascus, Robert S. Ford was active in challenging President Bashar al-Assad’s crackdown on the emerging political opposition in Syria.

2014-02-06.title: Preserving Routine Amid Rising Violence
2014-02-06.abstract: Bombings have ebbed but have never retreated over the years in the working-class Shaab area of Baghdad.

2014-02-06.title: Israel: Final Approval Is Given on East Jerusalem Apartments
2014-02-06.abstract: The 500 new apartments are in neighborhoods, in territory Israel captured during the 1967 war and later annexed, that are considered illegal settlements by most of the world.

2014-02-06.title: Jihadist Return Is Said to Drive Attacks in Egypt
2014-02-06.abstract: Egypt has been largely insulated from the Islamist violence in the region, but it is now facing increasing terrorism as Egyptians return from jihad abroad to fight the country’s military-backed government.

2014-02-06.title: Ukraine Chief Loses Support in Stronghold
2014-02-06.abstract: The defections of two prominent businessmen in eastern Ukraine, a stronghold for President Viktor F. Yanukovych, signal growing frustrations with him even among his base.

2014-02-06.title: ‘An American in Madras’ Traces Career of Unlikely Pioneer in Tamil-Language Films
2014-02-06.abstract: The documentary’s director, Karan Bali, said Ellis R. Dungan, who made movies in the southern state of Tamil Nadu during the 1930s and 1940, introduced innovative techniques to the fledgling Tamil film industry.

2014-02-07.title: West Bank: U.N. Staff Ends Strike
2014-02-07.abstract: The United Nations Relief and Works Agency, the body that provides assistance to Palestinian refugees, announced that a two-month strike by its employees in the West Bank had ended.

2014-02-07.title: Italy: Toxic Waste Law Enacted
2014-02-07.abstract: President Giorgio Napolitano signed an emergency law to tackle environmental crimes and the damage produced by the illegal dumping or burning of waste.

2014-02-07.title: France: Seized Ivory Destroyed
2014-02-07.abstract: In an effort to deter poachers and traffickers, France publicly destroyed a stock of about three tons of confiscated ivory from the tusks of African elephants on Thursday.

2014-02-07.title: Muchelney Under Water
2014-02-07.abstract: Dozens of homes are flooded and much of the west coast of England and Wales are under fresh flood alerts.

2014-02-07.title: Accustomed to Floods, but ‘Nothing Like This,’ in Southern England
2014-02-07.abstract: In parts of England, the wettest January on record has left whole villages flooded and residents cut off from vital services. And it is still raining.

2014-02-08.title: In Left-Leaning Norway, Its Wealthy ‘Fox’ Gleefully Swings to the Right
2014-02-08.abstract: Olav Thon, a Norwegian billionaire, made his fortune with furs but dresses plainly, and supports the political right in a welfare system.

2014-02-08.title: Protests Over Government and Economy Roil Bosnia
2014-02-08.abstract: Unemployed youths, disgruntled workers and others set fire to buildings in Sarajevo, the capital, and across the country on a fourth day of protests.

2014-02-08.title: Despite Signs of Progress, Security Issues Rise in Tunisia
2014-02-08.abstract: Security has become the most pressing issue in Tunisia as the country tries to move forward with its democratic transition and rescue its still declining economy.

2014-02-08.title: China: Tibetan Dies in Act of Protest
2014-02-08.abstract: A Tibetan man died after setting himself on fire in an act of protest against the Chinese government, according to information released Friday by Free Tibet, an advocacy group.

2014-02-08.title: Canada: Government Deletes Drawings, Posted Online, of a Secret Spying Site
2014-02-08.abstract: The government of Canada on Friday removed detailed architectural drawings of a secret electronic eavesdropping station that had been posted online.

2014-02-09.title: Taking On Thailand’s Crisis With a Bit of Western Bite
2014-02-09.abstract: “Shallow News in Depth,” founded by two Thai-Americans, uses acid-laced sarcasm in a style called “Jon Stewart on crack.”

2014-02-09.title: Anne Heyman, Who Rescued Rwandan Orphans, Dies at 52
2014-02-09.abstract: Ms. Heyman found in Israel’s system of youth villages a model for assisting the young survivors of the Rwandan genocide.

2014-02-09.title: Months After Deadly Fire, Owners of Bangladesh Factory Surrender to Court
2014-02-09.abstract: Delowar Hossain and his wife, Mahmuda Akther, the owners of the Tazreen factory, face homicide charges in the 2012 blaze, which killed 112 employees.

2014-02-09.title: Household Worker Trips Up Britain’s Immigration Minister
2014-02-09.abstract: Mark Harper, who had led a crackdown that urged illegal immigrants to “go home,” stepped down after revealing that his house cleaner of six years had been living in the country illegally.

2014-02-09.title: Israel Orders Airstrike Against Gaza Militant
2014-02-09.abstract: The target of the strike by the Israeli military, a Palestinian militant accused of firing rockets into Israel, was critically wounded, along with a bystander.

2014-02-10.title: Independent News Station, Feeling Kremlin’s Wrath, Asks ‘Why?’
2014-02-10.abstract: Cable television operators pulled the plug on the Dozhd channel after Russia’s ruling party said a poll on a Sunday evening talk show was unpatriotic.

2014-02-10.title: Iran Agrees to Provide Data on Its Detonators
2014-02-10.abstract: A deal reached with the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog called for additional inspections of known nuclear sites and more information about uranium mines and other facilities.

2014-02-10.title: Break in Siege Is Little Relief to Syrian City
2014-02-10.abstract: Aid convoys were attacked during a three-day cease-fire in Homs, highlighting the difficulties plaguing even modest humanitarian efforts in Syria’s civil war.

2014-02-10.title: Jewish Hospital a Fixture in Tehran
2014-02-10.abstract: Many were surprised when the government of President Hassan Rouhani donated $400,000 to the Dr. Sapir Hospital, except for the hospital staff and for the neighbors who use it.

2014-02-10.title: Tokyo Rejects Candidate Who Stressed Nuclear Halt
2014-02-10.abstract: In the governor’s race, voters chose a former health minister focused on local concerns over a former prime minister who campaigned on putting an end to atomically generated electricity.

2014-02-11.title: Pirate Radio Gives Voice to Syrian Opposition
2014-02-11.abstract: Over a dozen opposition radio stations broadcast into Syria, and some activists try to keep their signals, and themselves, alive by running operations from places like Turkey.

2014-02-11.title: Memories of Home as Hollande Visits United States
2014-02-11.abstract: President François Hollande of France toured Monticello, the home of Thomas Jefferson, who, President Obama said, was “a Francophile through and through.”

2014-02-11.title: U.S. Debates Drone Strike on American Terrorism Suspect in Pakistan
2014-02-11.abstract: The discussion about targeting a citizen, who is believed to be plotting terrorist attacks, is the first time officials have considered killing an American overseas since President Obama imposed limits on drone strikes in May.

2014-02-11.title: In West Bank Settlements, Israeli Jobs Are Double-Edged Sword
2014-02-11.abstract: Israeli companies in West Bank settlements employ thousands of Palestinians, entrenching the occupation while helping to feed families who oppose it.

2014-02-11.title: Majority of Americans Favor Ties With Cuba, Poll Finds
2014-02-11.abstract: In Florida, an even higher number approve of normalizing relations with Havana, according to the survey’s results, described as an unprecedented reflection of shifting American attitudes.

2014-02-12.title: U.N. Court, on Appeal, Acquits 2 Rwandans in 1994 Genocide
2014-02-12.abstract: Two high-ranking officers previously convicted in the massacre were found not guilty by the International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda.

2014-02-12.title: U.S. Military Denounces Afghanistan’s Planned Release of More Prisoners
2014-02-12.abstract: American military officials are waging an unusually public campaign against the plan to free 65 prisoners, considered dangerous insurgents, from the military prison in Bagram.

2014-02-12.title: Nuclear Issue in Limbo as Indecision Grips Japan
2014-02-12.abstract: Even many of those who support phasing out nuclear plants after the Fukushima disaster see a temporary need for them.

2014-02-12.title: Tiger Population Grows in India, as Does Fear After Attacks
2014-02-12.abstract: Conflicts with humans have arisen in the few places where the endangered animals have rebounded thanks to conservation efforts.

2014-02-12.title: State Dinner Guest List for Hollande Is Impressive, if Minus One
2014-02-12.abstract: When President François Hollande of France arrived at the White House for a dinner in his honor Tuesday evening, he was most conspicuous for traveling alone.

2014-02-13.title: 2 U.S.-Led Coalition Soldiers Are Killed by Attackers in Afghan Uniforms
2014-02-13.abstract: The deadly episode was the first reported instance in 2014 of what are called green-on-blue attacks, in which uniformed Afghans turn on their Western allies.

2014-02-13.title: Italian Premier’s Latest Challenge Comes From His Own Party
2014-02-13.abstract: Prime Minister Enrico Letta is again fighting to save his government, but this time, the pressure is coming from the leader of his Democratic Party.

2014-02-13.title: Opposition Outlines Plans for a Future Syria
2014-02-13.abstract: During a second round of peace talks in Geneva, Syria’s opposition presented its most detailed plan yet for a transition, but it did not mention removing President Bashar al-Assad.

2014-02-13.title: Also Cheering at the Olympics: Russian Critics
2014-02-13.abstract: Complaints over the expense of the Winter Games have given way temporarily to a focus on national pride.

2014-02-13.title: U.N. Report Says Progress for Women Is Unequal
2014-02-13.abstract: Strides in reproductive health, pay and literacy are noted, but the world’s poorest countries have not kept up, the report finds.

2014-02-14.title: Germans Propose Law to Ease Return of Art Looted by Nazis
2014-02-14.abstract: Officials on Friday proposed a law that would lift the country’s 30-year statute of limitations for certain cases involving stolen property.

2014-02-14.title: As Hate Crimes Rise, British Muslims Say They’re Becoming More Insular
2014-02-14.abstract: Backlash after the killing of a soldier by two Muslims last year and a rise in anti-immigrant feelings have made some Muslim residents feel unwelcome.

2014-02-14.title: A Russian Protest Zone Where Almost No One Registers a Complaint
2014-02-14.abstract: A park designated as the official protest site of the Winter Olympics in Sochi, whose main arenas are about 10 miles away, highlights how the Russian government often smothers dissent under a thick blanket of bureaucracy.

2014-02-14.title: Prominent Opposition Leader in Venezuela Is Blamed for Unrest
2014-02-14.abstract: Leopoldo López, a former mayor of a district of Caracas, said the government was responsible for the deadly violence during antigovernment protests.

2014-02-14.title: Indian Publisher Withdraws Book, Stoking Fears of Nationalist Pressure
2014-02-14.abstract: A retired headmaster’s campaign has led Penguin Books India to agree to destroy copies of a book deemed offensive to Hindus, prompting fears that an ideological shift is underway.

2014-02-15.title: Roots of Bosnian Protests Lie in Peace Accords of 1995
2014-02-15.abstract: The violence that erupted a week ago across Bosnia and Herzegovina can be traced to the 1995 Dayton Accords, which brought peace but imposed a dysfunctional government that has stymied progress.

2014-02-15.title: Political Star Rises on Vow to Upend Italy’s Old Order
2014-02-15.abstract: Matteo Renzi engineered the Italian prime minister’s removal and is poised to succeed him, but now he faces the challenge of shaking the country out of its malaise.

2014-02-15.title: Medicines Made in India Set Off Safety Worries
2014-02-15.abstract: India, the second-largest exporter of over-the-counter and prescription drugs to the United States, is coming under increased scrutiny by American regulators.

2014-02-15.title: 2 Companies to Destroy Chemicals in Syria’s Arsenal
2014-02-15.abstract: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons chose hazardous-waste disposal companies based in the United States and Finland.

2014-02-15.title: South China Sea Tensions a Backdrop to Kerry’s China Visit
2014-02-15.abstract: Chinese officials take exception to statements by a U.S. Navy admiral that Washington would fulfill its obligations under a mutual defense treaty to help the Philippines if it came under attack

2014-02-16.title: A New Resource for Buddhist Scholars
2014-02-16.abstract: The Tibetan Buddhist Resource Center opened its library, with 12,000 works, at the Southwest University for Nationalities in Chengdu, China, in October. Archivists plan to scan the texts digitally.

2014-02-16.title: After Winding Odyssey, Tibetan Texts Find Home in China
2014-02-16.abstract: An American scholar’s trove of 12,000 Tibetan-language texts has a new home, a lavishly decorated library on the campus of the Southwest University for Nationalities in Chengdu, China.

2014-02-16.title: Afghan Army’s Test Begins With Fight for Vital Highway
2014-02-16.abstract: A week with Afghan troops safeguarding Highway 1 offers glimpses of a road war in which powerful leaders on all sides battle for control of Afghanistan’s crucial artery.

2014-02-16.title: 18 Feared Dead in Nepalese Air Crash
2014-02-16.abstract: An official at the airport in Katmandu, the capital, blamed bad weather for the crash, saying it had been snowing for much of the day.

2014-02-17.title: A Berlusconi Reminder as Italy Faces Another Unelected Premier
2014-02-17.abstract: Matteo Renzi will become the prime minister and has promised to change election laws so that voters can have a clearer say about who becomes premier in the future.

2014-02-17.title: In Egypt, a Chasm Grows Between Young and Old
2014-02-17.abstract: Many young Egyptians say the government’s heavy-handed reaction to protests has hampered their ability to establish a role in shaping the nation’s future.

2014-02-17.title: Turnabout in Bolivia as Economy Rises From Instability
2014-02-17.abstract: With a growing economy, a reduced debt and an enviable rainy-day fund, Bolivia has become a nation whose fiscal policy is praised by international financial institutions.

2014-02-17.title: Merkel Backs Plan to Keep European Data in Europe
2014-02-17.abstract: After the revelations of U.S. spying, Chancellor Angela Merkel said she would raise the matter at a meeting with the French president this week.

2014-02-17.title: Tunisian B-Boys’ Biggest Battle: Keeping Youths From Extremism
2014-02-17.abstract: In a small town, where some say many young people are drifting into drugs or even militancy, break dancing and a youth center offer alternatives.

2014-02-18.title: Celebrations, and Unease, in Libya on Anniversary of Uprising
2014-02-18.abstract: Libyans took to the streets on the third anniversary of the revolt against Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi, though many said the country’s leaders had squandered the revolution.

2014-02-18.title: Venezuela Cites Unrest in Expelling 3 U.S. Officials
2014-02-18.abstract: The consular officials were accused of visiting Venezuelan universities to recruit students to organize antigovernment protests.

2014-02-18.title: Sudanese TV Show, Without the Cutthroat ‘You’re Fired,’ Rewards Entrepreneurs
2014-02-18.abstract: “Mashrouy,” Sudan’s version of “The Apprentice,” has kindled the imagination of the nation’s youth, many of whom are unemployed.

2014-02-18.title: U.S. Scolds Russia as It Weighs Options on Syrian War
2014-02-18.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry sharply criticized Russia’s support of President Bashar al-Assad of Syria, saying it was undermining prospects for a negotiated solution.

2014-02-18.title: Spain, Land of 10 P.M. Dinners, Asks if It’s Time to Reset Clock
2014-02-18.abstract: A pro-efficiency movement is pushing the country be more productive by aligning with Europe’s 9-to-5 schedule.

2014-02-19.title: After Delays, Musharraf Appears Before Pakistani Court
2014-02-19.abstract: Pervez Musharraf, the former military ruler of Pakistan, finally made an appearance before a special court that is deciding whether to try him for treason.

2014-02-19.title: Ex-U.S. Lawmaker Charged With Pornography Possession in Zimbabwe
2014-02-19.abstract: The arrest is the latest legal entanglement for former Representative Mel Reynolds of Illinois.

2014-02-19.title: South Sudan’s Forces Clash With Rebels Near U.N. Base
2014-02-19.abstract: A battle in an oil-rich part of South Sudan was the most significant violation of the nearly month-old cease-fire.

2014-02-19.title: Germany’s Coal Addiction
2014-02-19.abstract: Germany is in the midst of an unprecedented shift to renewable energy, but is still dependent on brown coal. The village of Atterwasch may become a victim of Germany’s hunger for brown coal.

2014-02-19.title: German Village Resists Plans to Strip It Away for the Coal Underneath
2014-02-19.abstract: Even as Germany tries to wean itself from fossil fuels and nuclear power, its hunger for coal threatens the existence of villages like Atterwasch that lie atop coal deposits.

2014-02-20.title: Hard Times Leave Some Greek Car Owners With Nothing but Rust
2014-02-20.abstract: Greeks have long had a love affair with cars, but lately, Greece has become a vast graveyard of abandoned automobiles, with motorists who can no longer afford to pay the taxes on their vehicles turning in piles of car plates.

2014-02-20.title: Disillusionment in Libya Over Vote on Charter Assembly
2014-02-20.abstract: Increasingly frustrated Libyans on Thursday will elect 60 lawmakers to write a new constitution after Parliament failed to appoint the charter-drafting body.

2014-02-20.title: Nationalistic Remarks From Japan Lead to Warnings of Chill With U.S.
2014-02-20.abstract: Comments criticizing the United States by associates of Japan’s prime minister have furthered concerns that a rift is growing between the two governments.

2014-02-20.title: Violence in Ukraine Creates Deepening Clash Between East and West
2014-02-20.abstract: As Russia denounced what it called a coup by extremists, the United States and Europe threatened to impose sanctions on those responsible for the violence — and each side accused the other of interference.

2014-02-20.title: Getting Stares on the Streets of Cambodia: Buses for the Masses
2014-02-20.abstract: Phnom Penh is experimenting with a public transportation system, beginning with 10 buses, in a program underwritten by the Japanese government.

2014-02-21.title: Russian Punk Group Ends Protest Tour of Sochi
2014-02-21.abstract: During a three-day stay in Sochi, members of Pussy Riot were detained three times, whipped by a Cossack and hounded by pro-government activists.

2014-02-21.title: New Russian Protest Video
2014-02-21.abstract: The Russian punk group Pussy Riot released a new music video protesting the human rights record of President Vladimir V. Putin during the Olympics.

2014-02-21.title: Syrian Rebels Sought to Bolster Coalition’s Case in Peace Talks
2014-02-21.abstract: Leaders from Syria’s insurgent coalition attended peace talks in Geneva with the aim of securing military backing from the United States and others.

2014-02-21.title: Haitian Dictator May Be Charged With Human Rights Crimes, Court Says
2014-02-21.abstract: An appeals court rejected an argument from Jean-Claude Duvalier, known as Baby Doc, that the statute of limitations had expired for the charge of crimes against humanity.

2014-02-21.title: Egypt Extends Its Crackdown to Journalists
2014-02-21.abstract: The military government has been detaining reporters, as well as other perceived enemies like activists and Islamists.

2014-02-22.title: David M. Herszenhorn Answers Questions on Ukraine
2014-02-22.abstract: Mr. Herszenhorn, a correspondent for The New York Times who has been covering the crisis since it began three months ago, offers insight.

2014-02-22.title: Syrians Seek New Delay in Export of Chemical Arms
2014-02-22.abstract: The Syrian government has asked for a delay until mid-May for the export of its chemical weapons arsenal, an indication that the country’s pledge to eradicate that arsenal is fraying.

2014-02-22.title: With Tumult Around Him, Russian Diplomat Keeps Calm
2014-02-22.abstract: Vitaly I. Churkin finds his country the focus of international attention both at home with the Olympics, and at the United Nations.

2014-02-22.title: In Struggles of Artist With His Work, Hints of Mexico’s Own
2014-02-22.abstract: Damián Ortega’s installation outside the new Museo Jumex in Mexico City has become a commentary on modern Mexico, in even more ways than he had intended.

2014-02-22.title: Video Notebook: Mexican Art
2014-02-22.abstract: The Times’s Damien Cave tracks the delayed debut of Damián Ortega’s modern art installation outside the new Museo Jumex in Mexico City.

2014-02-23.title: After Farmers Commit Suicide, Debts Fall on Families in India
2014-02-23.abstract: India’s small farmers, once the country’s economic backbone and most reliable vote bank, are increasingly being left behind.

2014-02-23.title: Mexican Drug Kingpin Is Captured
2014-02-23.abstract: Joaquín Guzmán Loera, “El Chapo,” one of Mexico’s top drug bosses and a Forbes list billionaire since 2009, was captured today in a Mexican resort after a 13-year manhunt.

2014-02-23.title: In Japan’s Drill With the U.S., a Message for Beijing
2014-02-23.abstract: American military officials insisted an annual exercise, called Iron Fist, had nothing to do with a dispute over islands, but this year, the Japanese team came packing.

2014-02-23.title: Lviv, in Western Ukraine, Mourns One of Its Own Killed in Kiev
2014-02-23.abstract: A university lecturer who was shot to death had made several trips east to the capital to take part in antigovernment protests, a friend said.

2014-02-23.title: Kerry Says Venezuela Crackdown Is ‘Unacceptable’
2014-02-23.abstract: As the demonstrations continued to grow, Secretary of State John Kerry strengthened his criticism of the Venezuelan government’s response to the protesters.

2014-02-24.title: Top Military Body Against Syria’s Assad Is in Chaos, Undermining Fight
2014-02-24.abstract: Islamist groups have seized the Supreme Military Council’s weapons, its members have stolen or sold its supplies and one prominent commander has joined an offshoot of Al Qaeda.

2014-02-24.title: How a Kingpin Above the Law Fell, Incredibly, Without a Shot
2014-02-24.abstract: The takedown of the world’s most wanted man — Joaquín Guzmán Loera, the head of a sophisticated narcotics enterprise — upended assumptions about the impunity of Mexican mobsters.

2014-02-24.title: Blast Kills 12 Riding in Van in Pakistan
2014-02-24.abstract: Sixteen others were injured in the blast in a town with a history of sectarian and militant violence in Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa Province.

2014-02-24.title: First Companies Give to Fund for Victims of Bangladeshi Factory Collapse
2014-02-24.abstract: Organizers called for other brands and retailers to join El Corte Inglés, Inditex, Loblaw, Mango and Mascot, with a goal of $40 million for the Rana Plaza disaster.

2014-02-24.title: As His Fortunes Fell in Ukraine, a President Clung to Illusions
2014-02-24.abstract: Between the signing ceremony for a peace deal and a break to congratulate Ukraine’s Olympic team, President Viktor F. Yanukovych’s prospects took a dramatic turn for the worse.

2014-02-25.title: Inside the ‘Museum of Corruption’
2014-02-25.abstract: In February 2014, the palatial compound of Viktor Yanukovych, the former president of Ukraine, was opened to the public after he fled Kiev.

2014-02-25.title: Scottish Oil and Gas an Issue in Vote on Independence
2014-02-25.abstract: Alex Salmond, Scotland’s first minister, who supports independence, and Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain, who does not, also differ on the future of oil and gas reserves in the North Sea.

2014-02-25.title: Israeli Warplanes Strike Near the Border of Syria and Lebanon
2014-02-25.abstract: Israel has struck Syria at least three times in the past year, according to United States officials, to prevent sophisticated weapons from reaching Hezbollah amid the chaos of Syria’s war.

2014-02-25.title: Wary Stance From Obama on Ukraine
2014-02-25.abstract: While George W. Bush was inspired by Ukraine’s 2004 revolution, Barack Obama has approached the unrest of 2014 with a more clinical detachment aimed at avoiding instability.

2014-02-25.title: Just Like His Power, Ukrainian Ex-Leader Vanishes Into Thin Air
2014-02-25.abstract: Accused of mass murder, Viktor F. Yanukovych has disappeared, along with a handful of loyalists and perhaps his girlfriend.

2014-02-26.title: Trust Eroded, Obama Looks Beyond Karzai
2014-02-26.abstract: President Obama is apparently resigned to dealing with the next Afghan president on troop withdrawals and training, since President Hamid Karzai has refused to sign a long-term security agreement.

2014-02-26.title: Turks Are Glued to a Sensational Drama, This One Political
2014-02-26.abstract: Although the Turkish period drama “The Magnificent Century” has become an obsession in the country, the show gripping many Turks is their nation’s own political crisis.

2014-02-26.title: Tentatively, European Union Weighs Its Options on Support for a New Ukraine
2014-02-26.abstract: The European Union’s foreign policy chief, Catherine Ashton, said at a news conference that Europe would offer “support, not interference,” and urged Ukrainians to maintain good ties with Russia.

2014-02-26.title: An Unfinished Ukraine Palace and a Fugitive Leader’s Folly
2014-02-26.abstract: Workers hired to build a retreat for Viktor F. Yanukovych have walked off the job, since he will never be able to pay them after his ouster as Ukraine’s president.

2014-02-26.title: Crude Weapons Help Fuel Unrest in Bastion of Venezuelan Opposition
2014-02-26.abstract: Protesters are using a variety of homemade weapons against troops, including miniature firebombs and Miguelitos, which are made from hoses festooned with nails.

2014-02-27.title: Hard Talk Aside, Little Desire by the West to Leave Afghanistan
2014-02-27.abstract: While President Obama and NATO officials have talked of a withdrawal by year’s end, Western officials worry that swaths of the country would fall back under Taliban control.

2014-02-27.title: Kerry Condemns Uganda’s Antigay Law
2014-02-27.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry on Wednesday condemned the antigay measure signed into law in Uganda this week, saying it is as serious a moral offense as anti-Semitism in 1930s Germany or apartheid in white-led South Africa.

2014-02-27.title: Shrugging Off Past Setbacks, Obama Plans Personal Role in Middle East Peace Bid
2014-02-27.abstract: President Obama will press Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel to agree to a framework for a conclusive round of peace talks, officials said.

2014-02-27.title: Prize Catch for Ukrainians at Boat Harbor: A Soggy Trove of Government Secrets
2014-02-27.abstract: Documents fished out of the water provide a portrait of the final weeks, days and hours of members of a besieged inner circle trying desperately to maintain their grip on the government.

2014-02-27.title: Tears and Tissues as Editor Testifies in Phone Hacking Case
2014-02-27.abstract: The tabloids Rebekah Brooks once edited might have paid six figures or more for details like those she has been divulging about her personal life during her trial on phone hacking charges.

2014-02-28.title: Political Party Ends Blockade of NATO Supply Route in Pakistan
2014-02-28.abstract: The opposition politician Imran Khan announced Thursday that his political party, Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf, which governs the northwestern Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa Province, would end its three-month blockade of a NATO supply route.

2014-02-28.title: Several Bombings Leave Dozens Dead in Iraq
2014-02-28.abstract: At least 38 people were killed and more than 50 were wounded Thursday in a series of bombings in Iraq, three of them in Shiite areas of Baghdad, Iraqi officials said.

2014-02-28.title: As Africans Surge to Europe’s Door, Spain Locks Down
2014-02-28.abstract: Large groups of sub-Saharan immigrants have been charging the fences into a Spanish enclave in Morocco, raising alarms over their efforts and the officials’ response.

2014-02-28.title: Naming of Officials in Ukraine Reflects Homage to Power of the Street
2014-02-28.abstract: The inclusion of popular protest figures in an interim government reflected an effort to pay tribute to the role of the street in dislodging a president from power.

2014-02-28.title: State Department Report Details Suppression of Human Rights Abroad
2014-02-28.abstract: The government’s annual human rights assessment notes abuses in Egypt, Iran, Russia and elsewhere.
